scores.correctness_notes: Covers the main concepts correctly (changes, tasks, statuses, state persistence, API wait endpoint, CLI commands, ops library). Missing: change structure fields (id, kind, summary, ready bool, spawn-time, ready-time, task progress with done/total counters), API polling via GET /v1/changes/{id} checking 'ready' boolean, API filtering with select=in-progress/all/ready, and notices/change-update mechanism.
scores.hallucination_notes: PEBBLE_PERSIST=never environment variable not in gold standard and likely fabricated. client.WaitChange() Go client method not in gold standard. process.wait() for exec() is plausible but not in gold standard and not what was asked about. --last=start flag for pebble tasks not mentioned in gold standard.
